lede.founded: 2005 (Vukelic-family acquisition; the business itself dates to 1933 as Northern Distributing). Parent Try-It Distributing founded 1928.
lede.industry: Wholesale beverage distribution — beer, wine, spirits, water, energy drinks and non-alcoholic beverages (Anheuser-Busch house)
lede.coverage: ~21 counties across upstate New York, from the Capital District north to Lake Placid and west into the Southern Tier / Twin Tiers
lede.size: ~300 employees ('stakeholders'); ~8 million cases annually; 2,500+ customers; ~50-55 delivery trucks
lede.narrative: Saratoga Eagle is the upstate-New York arm of the Vukelic family's beverage empire, an Anheuser-Busch wholesaler that traces back to Try-It Distributing, founded in Lackawanna in 1928 by Croatian immigrant Stephen L. 'The Chief' Vukelic as a Prohibition-era soft-drink bottler. The family acquired the Glens Falls-area distributorship (formerly Northern Distributing) in 2005, with Jeff Vukelic — Stephen's grandson — leaving the EVP role at Try-It to run it as President/CEO. Operating from a ~165,000-sq-ft Saratoga Springs facility, the company grew aggressively through 2019-2022 via the Plattsburgh Distributing, Minogue's, Northern Eagle (Oneonta) and Seneca Beverage (Elmira) acquisitions, roughly doubling its territory to 21 counties and lifting volume toward 8 million cases. It distributes Budweiser, Michelob, Labatt, Rolling Rock, Red Bull (via sister company Balkan Beverage) and a deep roster of regional craft brands. The wider Vukelic group, anchored by Try-It and chaired by Gene Vukelic until his death in August 2025, ranks among the nation's Top 50 beverage distributors.

timeline.title: Try-It Distributing founded in Lackawanna
timeline.body: Croatian-born Stephen L. 'The Chief' Vukelic, 27, begins bottling soft drinks at his Lackawanna, NY plant during Prohibition, delivering from a single truck branded 'Check That Thirst with Try-It Bottled Beverages.'

timeline.title: Repeal of Prohibition; first beer brand
timeline.body: With the 21st Amendment, Vukelic picks up a local beer, Phoenix, beginning Try-It's pivot from bottling to beer distribution. The same year, the Glens Falls-area business that becomes Saratoga Eagle starts operating as Northern Distributing.

timeline.title: Anheuser-Busch franchise acquired
timeline.body: Try-It adds Budweiser and Michelob, securing the Anheuser-Busch distribution rights that define the family as an 'AB house.'

timeline.title: Labatt added
timeline.body: Try-It picks up Labatt, deepening its import/Canadian portfolio alongside its growing AB business.

timeline.title: Gene Vukelic joins the family business
timeline.body: Eugene P. 'Gene' Vukelic (b. 1930), a Canisius and Le Moyne graduate and U.S. Marine Corps veteran, joins Try-It after stints at General Motors and Cottrell Bus Service, beginning the second-generation era.

timeline.title: Seneca Beverage founded in Elmira
timeline.body: John Potter founds Seneca Beverage Corp. in Elmira, NY — a Southern Tier AB distributor that the Vukelics will acquire decades later.

timeline.title: Balkan Beverage subsidiary launched
timeline.body: Try-It forms Balkan Beverage to distribute non-alcoholic products, notably Red Bull energy drinks and Arizona teas, across upstate New York.

timeline.title: Saratoga Eagle established
timeline.body: The Vukelic family acquires the Glens Falls / Saratoga Springs AB distributorship and rebrands it Saratoga Eagle Sales & Service. Jeff Vukelic leaves his EVP post at Try-It to become President/COO (later CEO).

timeline.title: Generational loss
timeline.body: Stephen G. Vukelic (1927-2010), a partner in Try-It Distributing, dies, marking a passing within the founding generation as leadership consolidates under Gene and his sons.

timeline.title: Plattsburgh Distributing acquired
timeline.body: Saratoga Eagle acquires Plattsburgh Distributing in Clinton County, extending its territory to the far northern Adirondacks and prompting a warehouse expansion.

timeline.title: Saratoga Springs warehouse expansion
timeline.body: A 30,000-sq-ft addition brings the Duplainville Road HQ to ~165,000 sq ft, completed mid-February 2020 to absorb the Plattsburgh volume.

timeline.title: Minogue's Beverage Centers acquired
timeline.body: In November 2021, Jeff and brother Paul Vukelic buy the Minogue's Beverage retail chain (Queensbury, Malta, Saratoga, Wilton) from Jack Minogue Jr., who lacked a succession plan; the century-old Minogue's name is retained.

timeline.title: Northern Eagle (Oneonta) & Seneca Beverage (Elmira) acquired
timeline.body: Saratoga Eagle acquires two AB distributors — Northern Eagle Beverages of Oneonta and Seneca Beverage of Elmira — adding eight counties (21 total), ~50-60 stakeholders, and pushing annual volume from under 6 million toward 8 million cases.

timeline.title: Craft partnership with Speckled Pig Brewery
timeline.body: Effective Feb. 27, 2023, Saratoga Eagle begins distributing Ballston Spa's Speckled Pig Brewing Co., starting with its New England-style IPA 'Restitution' — part of a broader local-craft 'Drink Upstate NY' push.

timeline.title: Patriarch Gene Vukelic dies at 94
timeline.body: Eugene P. 'Gene' Vukelic, Try-It chairman and the second-generation force who grew the company from 12 to 600+ employees and a national Top 50 distributor, dies August 3, 2025. Leadership rests with third generation (Paul, Jeff, Matt) and fourth (Greg, Christopher, the Mahoneys).

news.headline: Saratoga Eagle buys Minogue's Beverage retail business
news.summary: Jeff and Paul Vukelic acquire Jack Minogue Jr.'s four-store Minogue's Beverage Centers chain; the Vukelics' succession depth solved the 69-year-old's lack of an heir, and they agreed to keep the century-old Minogue's name.

news.headline: Elmira's Seneca Beverage to be sold after 50+ years of family ownership
news.summary: Seneca Beverage Corp. (founded 1968 by John Potter), run by siblings John Potter and Betsy Potter-Knapp, agrees to sell to Saratoga Eagle, citing the Vukelics' succession plan and shared AB supplier base.

news.headline: Saratoga Eagle expands by acquiring distributorships in Oneonta and Elmira
news.summary: Saratoga Eagle closes on Northern Eagle Beverages (Oneonta) and Seneca Beverage (Elmira), both AB distributors, adding eight counties for a 21-county footprint and lifting volume from under 6M toward 8M cases.

news.headline: Saratoga Eagle announces distribution agreement with Speckled Pig Brewery
news.summary: Saratoga Eagle partners with Ballston Spa's Speckled Pig Brewing Co. to distribute its craft beers across the region (launching with the IPA 'Restitution'), effective Feb. 27, 2023.

news.headline: The Still at Speckled Pig to bring craft spirits to Ballston Spa
news.summary: Saratoga Eagle distribution partner Speckled Pig expands into distilling and elevated pub fare with 'The Still at Speckled Pig,' underscoring the distributor's deepening local-craft relationships.

news.headline: Try-It Distributing chairman Gene Vukelic dies at 94
news.summary: Eugene P. 'Gene' Vukelic, the second-generation leader who built Try-It (parent of Saratoga Eagle) from 12 employees into a national Top 50 distributor, dies Aug. 3, 2025. A USMC veteran and major Buffalo philanthropist.

highlights: Regional craft beer, cider, wine & spirits

facilities.name: Saratoga Eagle HQ & distribution center
facilities.note: ~165,000 sq ft after 2020 expansion; main warehouse, sales and admin; serves the Capital District and Adirondack North Country.

facilities.name: Oneonta branch (former Northern Eagle Beverages)
facilities.note: Acquired 2022; ex-AB distributor serving the central NY / Catskills region.

facilities.name: Elmira branch (former Seneca Beverage)
facilities.note: Acquired 2022; Southern Tier coverage; founded 1968 by John Potter.

facilities.name: Plattsburgh branch (former Plattsburgh Distributing)
facilities.note: Acquired 2019; covers Clinton/Franklin counties and the far northern Adirondacks toward Lake Placid.

facilities.name: Minogue's Beverage Centers (retail)
facilities.note: Four-store retail beverage chain acquired Nov. 2021; original Minogue's name retained.

pullQuotes.text: We have grown up with the people who run the other beverage distributors in New York. They are family businesses like ours. When someone is ready to retire or decides it's time to get out of the business, they approach us.
pullQuotes.cite: Jeff Vukelic, on Saratoga Eagle's acquisition strategy (Saratoga Business Journal, 2022)
Jeff Vukelic, President & CEO, on his decision-making framework

people.name: Stephen L. 'The Chief' Vukelic
people.meta: Croatian immigrant; founded Try-It as a Prohibition-era soft-drink bottler in Lackawanna, 1928; added AB (1946) and Labatt (1949). Deceased (1989).

people.name: Eugene P. 'Gene' Vukelic
people.meta: b. 1930, d. Aug 3, 2025; USMC veteran; grew the group from 12 to 600+ employees and a national Top 50 distributor; established Saratoga Eagle. Deceased.

people.name: Stephen G. Vukelic
people.meta: 1927-2010; partner in the family firm during the second-generation era. Deceased.

people.name: Jeff Vukelic
people.meta: HWS '88 (economics & political science); joined family business 1992; ran Saratoga Eagle since the 2005 acquisition.

people.name: Paul Vukelic
people.meta: Runs the Buffalo-based parent Try-It; co-acquired Minogue's with brother Jeff.

people.name: Matt Vukelic
people.meta: Third-generation Vukelic in the family enterprise's leadership group.

people.name: Greg Vukelic
people.meta: Fourth-generation Vukelic; runs the Elmira operation acquired in 2022.

people.name: Christopher Vukelic
people.meta: Fourth-generation Vukelic in the family succession plan.

executives.name: Jeff Vukelic
executives.meta: Leads Saratoga Eagle; drove the 2019-2022 acquisition spree.

flags.description: Patriarch and second-generation leader Gene Vukelic died Aug 2025; the enterprise now depends on third-generation siblings (Paul, Jeff, Matt) and an emerging fourth generation. Multi-branch family ownership creates governance/continuity complexity.

Heavy reliance on Anheuser-Busch as the anchor franchise ('AB house') exposes the business to AB volume declines and supplier consolidation; partially mitigated by Red Bull (Balkan), Labatt and a growing craft/NA portfolio.
Four acquisitions in roughly three years (Plattsburgh 2019, Minogue's 2021, Northern Eagle & Seneca 2022) doubled territory to 21 counties; scale-up carries operational risk.
